TOOLTIP.REQUIRED_VALIDATORS: Select supervisors who must validate movements of this risk.
TOOLTIP.UNANIMITY: If checked, ALL selected validators must approve.

INFO.TITLE: Security Matrix
INFO.DESC: Define the required validation rules for each security level (from 1 to 5). Only users with a sufficient level are displayed for each tier.
INFO.LOGIC_DESC: During a movement, the system first checks the specific matrix of the departure Base. If no validator is defined for the required risk level, the system automatically falls back to the global matrix (Country Office).

SECURITY_MATRIX.NO_VALIDATORS: No eligible users for this level.
SECURITY_MATRIX.CASCADE_TOOLTIP: If checked, validators from lower levels will also be required

MAINTENANCE.TITLE: Maintenance Tracking

CONFIG.TITLE: Maintenance Configuration
CONFIG.ADD_SERVICE: Add a service (e.g., A, B, C...)

MAINTENANCE.TRACKING_INFO: This tab allows you to visualize the health status of each vehicle in your fleet relative to its maintenance obligations. It compares current mileage with configured services (A, B, C...) to determine the exact gap. Vehicles requiring immediate attention appear with a Critical or Overdue status.
MAINTENANCE.HOW_IT_WORKS_PREDICTIVE_TEXT:
The tool automatically analyzes the actual average kilometers driven by each vehicle between previous services to predict the exact date of its next service.
It also monitors real fuel consumption via the logbook in the background, and generates an overconsumption alert (in purple or red) if the actual average exceeds the manufacturer's theoretical value by 20%.

INFO.TITLE: User Management
INFO.DESC: This page allows you to manage system access. You can create new users, assign them a role defined in the Matrix, and assign them a security validation level. Note: the list of available roles depends on your own permissions.

GUIDE.TITLE: How to calculate activity-weighted evolution (IAP)?
GUIDE.PRINCIPLE_TEXT: To compare two years fairly, we use the Activity Weighted Index (IAP). It allows measuring if consumption reduction is real (efficiency) or just due to lower activity.
GUIDE.COMPARISON_TEXT: We track the evolution of the ratio: Liters Consumed / IAP.
GUIDE.COMPARISON_EXPLANATION: If this ratio decreases by the target, it means you have become more energy efficient at equal activity.
GUIDE.EXAMPLE: Example: If your activity doubles (IAP x2) but your consumption increases by less than double, your ratio drops = Objective reached!
GUIDE.REMINDER: Make sure to fill in 'Activity' indicators every month in the Data Entry tab for the calculation to work.

WEIGHTS.TITLE: Activity Index Weighting (Recommended)
WEIGHTS.HINT: These weights determine the importance of each activity in your IAP score calculation.

ANALYSIS.TITLE: Energy Efficiency (Liters / IAP)
ANALYSIS.SUBTITLE: The goal is to be under the green line. If activity rises, consumption can rise, but this ratio must drop.

DASHBOARD.WELCOME_DESC: This is your personal dashboard. Here you will find all important data at a glance.

WEEKLY_CHECKLIST.DESCRIPTION: This tab centralizes the weekly safety inspections carried out by drivers from their mobile app. You can verify that each vehicle has been checked on time. Anomalies (brakes, tires, leaks...) reported by drivers are directly visible to trigger a maintenance action.
WEEKLY_CHECKLIST.TITLE: Weekly Checklist Tracking

PLANNED_INTERVENTIONS.DESCRIPTION: This section lists all manual interventions and specific requests for downtime for repair or verification. You can create, edit, or delete these events. "Completed" statuses indicate that the intervention is closed and the vehicle is available again.
PLANNED_INTERVENTIONS.TITLE: Maintenance History and Forecasts

SMART_COST_TAB.DESCRIPTION: This section financially analyzes your fleet. It consolidates fuel, repair, insurance, and acquisition costs to calculate the overall and unit cost per vehicle and per kilometer. Charts help you identify the most expensive vehicles to maintain.
SMART_COST_TAB.SUBTITLE: Financial and predictive analysis of your fleet (Last 30 Days)
SMART_COST_TAB.BASED_ON: Based on your future planned services (Km) and historical costs (Services A/B/C).

INTERVENTION.SECTION_TEST: Group test for 5 mins after intervention
INTERVENTION.TEST_BAD: Malfunction, the group can no longer be used.

GUIDE.TITLE: Generators Internal Maintenance Guide
GUIDE.INFO: This guide lists all maintenance tasks to be performed on generators and engines based on their operating hours. When creating a preventive intervention, these tasks will be automatically proposed as a checklist for the technician to validate.

SERVICES.A_DESC: Basic preventive maintenance, including visual inspection, adjustments, and primary oil change.

A_TASKS.1: Adjust battery terminals and battery securing
A_TASKS.3: Check overall condition, cleanliness, cleaning of liquid stains
A_TASKS.5: Check for oil, fuel, coolant leaks
A_TASKS.6: Drain primary fuel filter (& secondary) water separator
A_TASKS.8: Check battery electrolyte level (and top up if necessary)
A_TASKS.9: Drain engine oil & fill with adapted new oil
A_TASKS.12: Clean oil breather hose or tray

SERVICES.B_DESC: Intermediate preventive maintenance, including full replacement of filters and hoses check.

B_TASKS.0: All tasks from Service A (250 Hours)

SERVICES.C_DESC: Extensive preventive maintenance, targeting valves and cooling system.

C_TASKS.0: All tasks from Service A & B (250h and 500h)
C_TASKS.1: Check and adjust valve clearance (if necessary)

SERVICES.D_DESC: Major preventive maintenance, intervention on wearing parts and combustion chambers.

D_TASKS.0: All tasks from Service A, B & C (250h, 500h and 1000h)
D_TASKS.1: Test and/or change fuel injectors / Change all belts
